overview: Green Eldrazi in Modern leverages land recovery and mana acceleration to deploy massive creatures ahead of curve. The deck focuses on recovering lands from the graveyard to maintain tempo and overwhelm opponents with raw power. Signature cards like Thought-Knot Seer and Glaring Fleshraker enable this aggressive ramp strategy.
win_conditions: Deploying large Eldrazi creatures early via mana acceleration
Overwhelming opponents with combat damage from oversized threats
Maintaining board presence through continuous land recovery

keep_criteria: Hands with early land recovery spells like Kozilek's Command
Playable one-drops or early land recoverers on curve
Sufficient early land drops (4+ cards in hand)
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking land recovery or acceleration
Hands with only high-cost threats and no early plays
Hands with too many lands and no spells

early_game: Play lands and immediately use land recovery spells to accelerate
Deploy early threats or Thought-Knot Seer to establish board presence
Prioritize mana efficiency to reach turn three or four drops
mid_game: Continue recovering lands to maintain tempo against midrange decks
Play medium-sized Eldrazi to apply consistent pressure
Use Kozilek's Command for removal or card advantage when ahead
late_game: Deploy massive Eldrazi threats to close out the game
Focus on combat damage rather than card draw
Use remaining land recovery to stabilize against control answers

key_cards.name: Thought-Knot Seer
key_cards.role: Early land recovery and board presence
key_cards.notes: Play on curve to recover a land from the graveyard, accelerating into larger threats while providing a body to block or attack.

key_cards.name: Kozilek's Command
key_cards.role: Versatile two-drop for acceleration, removal, or value
key_cards.notes: Use the land recovery mode early to ramp, switch to removal or draw when ahead, and generate tokens to protect larger threats.

key_cards.name: Glaring Fleshraker
key_cards.role: Land recovery engine and early threat
key_cards.notes: Return lands to hand to replay them for untapped mana, enabling faster casting of Eldrazi while maintaining a 2/2 body on the battlefield.

sequencing_tips: Prioritize land recovery on turns one and two to enable three and four mana threats
Hold up mana for potential counterspells if facing control, otherwise play threats on opponent's turn
Use Kozilek's Command removal to clear blockers before attacking with large Eldrazi

matchups.advice: Race their hand size and use land recovery to play threats ahead of curve
Use early land recovery to deploy large blockers and finish quickly
Maintain tempo with continuous land recovery and apply steady pressure
